an1.51:1.2: And it is defiled by adventitious defilements.
an1.51:1.3: The uninstructed ordinary person does not truly know this as it is.
an1.51:1.4: Therefore I say that there is no development of the mind for the uninstructed ordinary person."
an1.52:1.2: And it is freed from adventitious defilements.
an1.52:1.3: The instructed noble disciple truly knows this as it is.
an1.52:1.4: Therefore I say that there is development of the mind for the instructed noble disciple."
an1.53:1.1: "If, bhikkhus, a bhikkhu cultivates a mind of goodwill for even as long as a finger-snap;
an1.53:1.3: 'the bhikkhu dwells not empty of meditation, he does the Master's instruction, he does the admonition, and he partakes of the almsfood of the country not in vain.'
an1.53:1.4: What then to say of those who make it abundant."
an1.54:1.1: "If, bhikkhus, a bhikkhu develops a mind of goodwill for even as long as a finger-snap;
an1.54:1.3: 'the bhikkhu dwells not empty of meditation, he does the Master's instruction, he does the admonition, and he partakes of the almsfood of the country not in vain.'
an1.54:1.4: What then to say of those who make it abundant."
an1.55:1.1: "If, bhikkhus, a bhikkhu attends to a mind of goodwill for even as long as a finger-snap;
an1.55:1.3: 'the bhikkhu dwells not empty of meditation, he does the Master's instruction, he does the admonition, and he partakes of the almsfood of the country not in vain.'
an1.55:1.4: What then to say of those who make it abundant."
an1.56:1.1: "Bhikkhus, I do not see any other single thing by which unarisen unwholesome _dhammas_ arise and arisen wholesome _dhammas_ decline as much as heedlessness.
an1.56:1.2: For one who is heedless, bhikkhus, unarisen unwholesome _dhammas_ arise, and arisen wholesome _dhammas_ decline."
an1.57:1.1: "Bhikkhus, I do not see any other single thing by which unarisen wholesome _dhammas_ arise and arisen unwholesome _dhammas_ decline as much as diligence.
an1.57:1.2: For one who is diligent, bhikkhus, unarisen wholesome _dhammas_ arise, and arisen unwholesome _dhammas_ decline."
an1.58:1.1: "Bhikkhus, I do not see any other single thing by which unarisen unwholesome _dhammas_ arise and arisen wholesome _dhammas_ decline as much as negligence.
an1.58:1.2: For one who is lazy, bhikkhus, unarisen unwholesome _dhammas_ arise, and arisen wholesome _dhammas_ decline."
an1.59:1.1: "Bhikkhus, I do not see any other single thing by which unarisen wholesome _dhammas_ arise and arisen unwholesome _dhammas_ decline as much as diligence.
an1.59:1.2: For one who is diligent, bhikkhus, unarisen wholesome _dhammas_ arise, and arisen unwholesome _dhammas_ decline."
an1.60:1.1: "Bhikkhus, I do not see any other single thing by which unarisen unwholesome _dhammas_ arise and arisen wholesome _dhammas_ decline as much as laziness.
an1.60:1.2: For one who is lazy, bhikkhus, unarisen unwholesome _dhammas_ arise, and arisen wholesome _dhammas_ decline."
